entities.name: V (Vrijen)
entities.context: Career coach providing guidance to Daniel, founder of Careerspan

entities.name: Daniel
entities.context: Person in V's network receiving career coaching and advice

entities.name: Positive-EV Framing
entities.context: V's framework for treating indie/side projects as legitimate career moves with expected positive value

entities.name: Compromise on Your Soul
entities.context: V's insight that modern job search requires presenting oneself in artificial ways that feel inauthentic

entities.name: Two-Resume Strategy
entities.context: Strategy V advises, referenced alongside a dating analogy for job search positioning

entities.name: Long-Tail Value of Relationships
entities.context: Principle that past network connections retain value over time and can resurface as opportunities

entities.name: ATS-Friendly Bullet Points
entities.context: Resume formatting technique V frames as hoops to jump through rather than reflections of worth

entities.name: Careerspan
entities.context: V's company, context for his career coaching practice

relationships.context: V is coaching Daniel on career strategy, job search, and indie project framing
V encourages treating side projects as legitimate career moves that keep skills sharp and create artifacts
V holds the honest view that modern job search requires artificial self-presentation
V advises a two-resume approach as part of his coaching methodology
V's reconnection with Daniel reinforces his belief in the enduring value of network relationships
Daniel is in V's network and still sees him as strong talent, demonstrating long-tail relationship value
ATS optimization is an example of the artificial presentation V describes as soul-compromising
Both concepts support V's broader philosophy that unconventional moves and past connections compound over time
